info.title: Printr API
info.description:
Printr is the first chain-abstracted token creation platform that enables seamless deployment
and trading across many blockchain networks. Built on Axelar Network's and LayerZero's
cross-chain infrastructure, Printr eliminates the complexity of multi-chain token management by
providing a unified interface for creating, discovering, and trading tokens across any
supported blockchain.

The API provides programmatic access to Printr's core functionality, enabling developers to
integrate omni-chain token creation capabilities into their applications. Whether you're
building a DeFi platform, marketplace, or trading interface, the Printr API handles all
cross-chain (x-chain) complexity behind the scenes.

Key features:
- Create tokens on multiple chains simultaneously with a single API call
- Unified liquidity across all supported networks through automated bonding curves
- X-chain trading and arbitrage capabilities
- Real-time deployment status tracking across chains
- Comprehensive token discovery and analytic
- X-chain price charts

**This API is currently in beta. Breaking changes may occur as we refine the platform based on
partner feedback.**

tags.name: Creation
tags.description:
Create and deploy tokens across multiple blockchain networks. Leveraging technologies like
Axelar Network's Interchain Token Service (ITS) and LayerZero's cross-chain messaging,
Printr provides seamless x-chain token deployment with unified liquidity management.

## Home Chain and Cascading Deployments
Every token has a "home chain" (also called "origin chain") where the initial creation
transaction occurs. From this home chain, deployments cascade to additional chains through
one of our supported x-chain protocol providers.

_DEV NOTE: We may rename "home chain" to "origin chain" (or something akin) in the future
for a more intuitive understanding to the consumers of this API._

**During Printr's beta period, the graduation threshold for each chain is locked at $1,000
USD.**

tags.name: Discovery
tags.description: Explore and discover tokens across the Printr ecosystem.

post.description:
Calculates comprehensive costs for creating a token across multiple blockchain networks before
committing to the creation process.

## Quote Calculation
The quote includes:
- **Deployment Costs**: Gas fees for deploying contracts on each selected chain
- **X-Chain Messaging Fees**: X-chain protocol fees for coordinating deployments
- **Initial Buy Costs**: Amount required for the initial token purchase based on your configuration

## Chain Specification
The first chain in the array becomes the "home chain" where the primary deployment occurs.
Additional chains receive deployments through cascading x-chain messages.

chains.description:
Array of CAIP-2 chain identifiers where the token will be deployed.
The first indexed chain will be treaded as the home chain for the
deployment transaction.

graduation_threshold_per_chain_usd.description: The graduation threshold (bonding curve size) per chain in USD. Min: $15,000, max: $1,000,000. Defaults to $69,000 USD.

custom_fees.description:
Optional custom fee configuration.
Fees are in basis points (100 bps = 1%).
If omitted, defaults are used.

fee_sink.description:
Where telecoin fees are directed.
- dev: Creator wallet receives fees (default)
- stake_pool: Proof of Belief staking pool rewards stakers
- buyback: Buy back and burn tokens (Solana only)
- liquidity_pool: Compound fees into LP (Solana only, post-graduation)
Defaults to "dev" if omitted.

telecoin_supply_on_curve_ratio_bps.description:
Ratio of the telecoin supply sold on the bonding curve, in basis points.
The remainder is deposited into the AMM after graduation.
Can be used to control initial market cap.
See docs for formulas.
Omit or use 0 for a default.

max_telecoin_supply.description:
The maximum total telecoin supply across all chains.
Defaults to 1 billion if omitted.

post.description:
Initiates the creation of a new token with simultaneous deployment across multiple blockchain
networks. This endpoint orchestrates the entire token creation process from initial deployment
to x-chain propagation.

## Token Creation Process
1. **Validation**: Verifies token metadata and deployment parameters
2. **X-Chain Preparation**: Prepares Axelar messages for remote deployments
3. **Payload Generation**: Creates the transaction calldata for the home chain
4. **Catalog Registration**: Registers the token in Printr's discovery system

## Creator Account and Home Chains
The creator account is a CAIP-10 account identifier that couples a chain identifier and a
specific address. The single field `creator_account` is used to specify both the creators
wallet address, in addition to the expected home chain for the token.

## Transaction Execution
The response includes transaction calldata that must be:
1. Signed by the creator's wallet specified in the creator_account field
2. Submitted to the home chain
3. Confirmed on-chain to trigger cascading deployments

## Image Requirements
Token images must be:
- Base64 encoded
- Maximum 500KB in size
- Square aspect ratio recommended
- GIF, PNG, or JPEG format

get.description:
Retrieves comprehensive information about a token including metadata and immutable properties of
the bonding curve.

## Cross-Chain Token Identity
Each token has a unique x-chain identifier that remains consistent across all blockchain deployments.
This enables unified tracking regardless of which chain you're querying from.

## Identifier
This endpoint accepts two formats for the `id` parameter.

Either Printr specific **Telecoin ID** (hex string). This is a cross-chain unique identifier for the Printr token.
Alternatively, use **Token Contract Address** (CAIP-10 format). This is the token's contract address on a specific chain (<https://chainagnostic.org>)

_DEV NOTE: the token identity description may change when we integrate x-chain Solana deployments._

id.description: Cross-chain unique identifier for the token

creatorAddresses.description: List of creator addresses for the token

chains.description: List of chains where token is deployed

homeChain.description: The origin chain which was used to propagate the other remote creations.

basePairAddresses.description: List of base pair addresses for the token

referenceAssetId.description: The reference asset ID for the token

maxSupplyExponent.description: The maximum supply exponent for the token

completionThresholdBasisPoints.description: The completion threshold basis points for the token

graduationThresholdRefAtomic.description: The graduation threshold reference atomic for the token

initialPriceRefAtomic.description: The initial price reference atomic for the token

get.description:
Monitors the real-time deployment status of a token across all target blockchain networks.
Essential for tracking the cascading deployment process after token creation.

## Deployment Lifecycle
Each chain deployment progresses through these states:
1. `**PENDING**`: The token has been cataloged by the Printr platform, however, we have not seen
    any associayed on-chain activity related to the token yet.
2. `**DEPLOYING**`: X-chain message sent via one of our x-chain protocol providers, and we're
    awaiting confirmation that the deployment has suceeded.
3. `**LIVE**`: The token has been deployed to the target chain and is ready for trading.
4. `**FAILED**`: Deployment unsuccessful (with error details)

## X-Chain Messaging
For remote chain deployments, track:
- **Transport Provider**: X-chain messaging protocol (e.g., AXELAR, CORAL, LAYERZERO)
- **Message ID**: The message identifier relevant to the transport protocol.

## Identifier
This endpoint accepts two formats for the `id` parameter.

Either Printr specific **Telecoin ID** (hex string). This is a cross-chain unique identifier for the Printr token.
Alternatively, use **Token Contract Address** (CAIP-10 format). This is the token's contract address on a specific chain (<https://chainagnostic.org>)

bearerAuth.description:
Static JWT tokens will be used during development.

This will be substituted for OAuth2 client creds in future.

CAIP_2_Chain.description:
Chain Agnostic Improvement Proposal (CAIP-2) blockchain identifier. Provides a standardized format for identifying any blockchain network regardless of its underlying technology stack. Format: {namespace}:{reference}

Examples:
- eip155:1 - Ethereum Mainnet
- eip155:137 - Polygon
- eip155:8453 - Base
- solana:5eykt4UsFv8P8NJdTREpY1vzqKqZKvdp - Solana Mainnet

VariableInitialBuy.description:
Configures the initial token purchase executed during creation. This purchase bootstraps
liquidity in the bonding curve and determines the starting price point. Exactly one
purchase method must be specified.

**Note:** The initial buy is capped to 69% of the total supply.

Options:
- supply_percent: Purchase a percentage of total token supply
- spend_usd: Purchase tokens worth a specific USD amount
- spend_native: Purchase using a specific amount of native tokens

supply_percent.description: Percentage of total supply to purchase (0.01-69)

spend_usd.description: USD amount to spend on initial purchase (must be less than 69% of the total supply)

spend_native.description: Atomic native token amount to spend (must be less than 69% of the total supply)

chains.description:
Array of CAIP-2 chain identifiers where the token will be deployed.
The first indexed chain will be treaded as the home chain for the
deployment transaction.

graduation_threshold_per_chain_usd.description: The graduation threshold (bonding curve size) per chain in USD. Min: $15,000, max: $1,000,000. Defaults to $69,000 USD.

custom_fees.description:
Optional custom fee configuration.
Fees are in basis points (100 bps = 1%).
If omitted, defaults are used.

fee_sink.description:
Where telecoin fees are directed.
- dev: Creator wallet receives fees (default)
- stake_pool: Proof of Belief staking pool rewards stakers
- buyback: Buy back and burn tokens (Solana only)
- liquidity_pool: Compound fees into LP (Solana only, post-graduation)
Defaults to "dev" if omitted.

telecoin_supply_on_curve_ratio_bps.description:
Ratio of the telecoin supply sold on the bonding curve, in basis points.
The remainder is deposited into the AMM after graduation.
Can be used to control initial market cap.
See docs for formulas.
Omit or use 0 for a default.

max_telecoin_supply.description:
The maximum total telecoin supply across all chains.
Defaults to 1 billion if omitted.

CAIP_10_Account.description:
Chain Agnostic Improvement Proposal (CAIP-10) account identifier. Provides a universal format for identifying accounts across different blockchain networks. Format: {namespace}:{reference}:{account_address}

Examples:
- eip155:1:0xc33f294bae8f55554c7b786306eac562d8fe9566 - Ethereum address
- solana:5eykt4UsFv8P8NJdTREpY1vzqKqZKvdp:7S3P4HxJpyyigGzodYwHtCxZyUQe9JiBMHyRWXArAaKv - Solana address

Asset.description: A representation of a token or asset associated with a specific chain.

id.description: Reference asset identified as a CAIP-10 Account ID.

symbol.description: Symbol used to identify the denomination of the asset.

decimals.description: The number of decimals used to represent the asset.

price_usd.description: The estimated price of the asset in USD.

asset_id.description: Reference asset identified as a CAIP-10 Account ID

cost_asset_atomic.description: Atomic cost in the reference asset.

limit.description: Upper bound spending limit for the cost (if applicable).

Quote.description: Comprehensive cost breakdown for token creation across selected chains. Includes all fees required for deployment, cross-chain messaging, and initial liquidity provision. Use this information to ensure sufficient funds before initiating token creation.

router.description: The routing provider / type simulating the quote.

assets.description: List of assets involved in the quote.

initial_buy_amount.description: The token amount transfered to the creation as part of the initial buy.

message.example: The specified chain 'eip155:999' is not supported

Optional external links related to the telecoin.
Empty strings are ignored.

to.description: The CAIP-10 account identifier to send the transaction to.

is_signer.description: Whether the account must sign the transaction.

is_writable.description: Whether the account is writable by the program.

accounts.description: The accounts to pass to the program in the order they are expected. Can be empty.

data.description: Serialized instruction params as a base64 string.

mint_address.description: The expected address of the telecoin mint.

ixs.description: List of Solana instructions to include in the transaction.

lookup_table.description: Use this lookup table to minimize the tx size.

TokenDeployment.description: Real-time status information for a token's deployment on a specific blockchain. Tracks the complete lifecycle from initial deployment through to trading availability, including cross-chain message propagation for remote chains.

BadRequest.description:
The request contains invalid parameters or violates API constraints. Common causes:
- Invalid CAIP-2 chain identifier format
- Unsupported blockchain network specified
- Missing required fields in request body
- Invalid initial buy configuration (e.g., percentage > 100%)
- Token metadata exceeds size limits

error.message: The specified chain 'invalid-chain' is not a valid CAIP-2 identifier

Unauthorized.description:
Authentication failed or required credentials are missing. Ensure:
- Valid OAuth2 token is included in the Authorization header
- Token has required scopes for the requested operation
- Token has not expired

RateLimited.description:
Request rate limit exceeded. The API implements rate limiting to ensure fair usage:
- Check X-RateLimit headers for limit details
- Implement exponential backoff for retries
- Consider caching responses where appropriate

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when rate limit resets

error.message: Rate limit exceeded. Please retry after 1642338000

InternalError.description:
An unexpected error occurred processing the request. These are typically transient issues:
- Retry with exponential backoff
- Check Printr status page for system-wide issues
- Contact support if errors persist with request_id for debugging

NotFound.description:
The requested resource does not exist. This can occur when:
- Token ID does not match any created tokens
- Token exists but has no deployments yet
- Attempting to access tokens from other platforms not yet indexed
